steps.text: Initiate and develop proposed NPIs within the scope of authority or affecting program operations.
Request review and clearance by appropriate directorates, regional offices, Office of the Solicitor (SOL), the Assistant Secretary, etc.
Provide proposed NPI and necessary background information to ensure the proposed issuance is thoroughly understood and considered.
Establish a due date for response return.
Review and consider comments received and address them as appropriate.

Refer to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary any unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the substance or potential policy implications of the proposed NPI or to the priority accorded to the proposed NPI’s review.
Ensure that systems for receiving, reviewing, tracking, concurring, and issuing NPIs are effective for consistently complying with requirements of this instruction.

preconditions.text: There are unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the substance or potential policy implications of the proposed NPI or to the priority accorded to the proposed NPI's review that emerged during the clearance process.

constraints.text: Allow no more than three weeks turnaround time for response return.

steps.text: Routinely review published NPIs to ensure information accurately reflects the Agency’s current policies and procedures.
Update or archive outdated NPIs as appropriate.

preconditions.text: If NPIs are outdated

steps.text: Complete items 1 through 8 on the OSHA 201.
Determine whether field coordination is necessary.
Allow a minimum of 15 working days for review and clearance.
Prepare an electronic version of the OSHA 201 and a draft directive to provide electronically to all clearance officials and proxies.
Identify all clearance officials and proxies who will receive a copy.
Obtain the approval of your Program Director and send copies to the clearance officials and proxies.
Review and reconcile any comments noted in item 8 of the OSHA 201 by clearance officials/proxies in the National Office and in the field.
Contact nonresponsive clearance offices prior to the review deadline to determine whether clearance/comments will be submitted by the requested deadline.

preconditions.text: If a shorter clearance period is desired, consult with the directives officer.

constraints.text: Directions are a minimum of 5 working days for review and clearance.

steps.text: Review the draft directive. If necessary, request an extension from the originator as soon as possible.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator. Alternatively, sends concurrence or comments electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.).
Negotiate the acceptance of comments with the originator or originating Program Director or presents the case to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate for approval clearance.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and requests the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Coordinate union review through the Labor Management Relations Officer.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the union, delayed posting request, or pending press release.
Provide the final 508-complaint PDF file directive to the web administrator for posting to the Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage within the requested timeframe.
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.

preconditions.text: Before a direction may be drafted, the originator will convene a meeting with directorates and regional offices to provide information on the content of the direction and provide an opportunity for discussion of initial questions and issues related to the content of the direction.

Before a direction may go into effect, the originator must provide a copy of the proposed direction to each clearance office required to provide clearance if the direction was issued as an instruction.

If a resolution is unable to be reached, outstanding issue(s) are elevated to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary, and as necessary, to the Assistant Secretary, for a decision regarding the issue of contention.

constraints.text: Clearance offices have up to five working days from the date of the OSHA 201 to consider the proposed direction and provide comments.

steps.text: Prepare the direction clearance packet.
Submit the packet, along with a request for the approval and signature of the Assistant Secretary, to the directives officer for processing.
Provide a 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer.
Coordinate the publication of the direction with the web administrator taking into consideration a delayed release or press release.
Review the direction to determine whether the policy contained therein will be continued and take necessary steps to ensure it is replaced with a cleared instruction if needed as soon as possible.
Contact the clearance official in question to negotiate a satisfactory extension for review or meet to resolve any issues, questions, or concerns.
Notify OAS of extension request or other delays in clearance.
Schedule a meeting with the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary to resolve the matter.
Elevate outstanding issues to the Assistant Secretary for a decision on whether to issue the directive, revise, reconsider, or defer action.

preconditions.text: Once all comments are resolved
Upon approval of the Assistant Secretary
Six months after issuance
If a clearance office fails to return the OSHA 201 by the deadline
If there is a failure to achieve an agreement on comments from clearance offices
Absent resolution of the issue(s) from the Deputy Assistant Secretary

constraints.text: Prior to OAS submission, non-concurrence forms must have update to reflect concurrence.
The originating Program Director/Regional Administrator will not contact the union directly.
Communication will be between the Office of Employee and Labor Management Relations and the Office of Audit.

steps.text: Submit publication requests to the web administrator.
Submit final unsigned directives to the directives officer.

preconditions.text: All final unsigned directives must be 508 compliant.

constraints.text: A PDF file that can be saved and viewed offline must be available for each directive.

steps.text: Review non-substantive changes by the Program Director (or Regional Administrator), directives officer, and the Office of the Solicitor.

Review and approve non-significant changes by the Program Director (or Regional Administrator), expert directorates, Directorate of Administrative Programs, directives officer, Assistant Secretary's Office, and the Office of the Solicitor.
Submit significant changes through the normal clearance process.
Cancel the directive either by reference in a new/revised instruction, notice, or direction, or by issuing a specific cancellation notice.

preconditions.text: The change is non-substantive (corrects typos, grammar, syntax, minor inaccuracies, or outdated references).
The change is non-significant (does not significantly alter the requirements of the directive).
The change is significant (major substantive changes altering purpose, intent, policies, or procedures).
If updating FOM, SHMS, or OTM, only the chapter being updated must be sent through the clearance process.

constraints.text: Full clearance as described in Chapter 3 is not required for non-significant changes.

steps.text: Prepare a cancellation notice using the cancellation template on the OSHA Directives Tools webpage.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director.
Provide the cancellation notice and concurrence grid providing review by the originating directorate, the directives officer, and the OAS.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting to the directives webpage.
Review the cancellation packet to determine if complete and accurate for the approval process.
Provide revision request from the OAS to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and request the cancellation notice be formatted to 508-compliant PDF.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the Assistant Secretary.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file to the web administrator for posting to the OSHA Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF to OSHA Directives webpage within requested

preconditions.text: Approval by the Assistant Secretary

steps.text: Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Archive directives containing an expiration date.
Provide notification to national office directors and regional administrators of expiring directives for the upcoming fiscal year in quarter four of the current fiscal year.
Archive cancellation notices three months after the effective date.

preconditions.text: Directive contains an expiration date
It is quarter four of the current fiscal year
Three months have elapsed since the effective date of the cancellation notice

steps.text: Follow the required review process for non-substantive changes.
Follow the required review process for non-significant changes.
Follow the normal clearance process for significant changes.
Refer to Appendix A for national clearance process.

preconditions.text: The changes are non-substantive (correcting typos, grammar, syntax, minor factual inaccuracies, or updating outdated references).
The changes are non-significant (do not significantly alter the requirements of the directive).
The changes are significant (major substantive changes that change the purpose or intent of the directive, or substantially alter OSHA policies and procedures).
